Ships: Ships. I did a search for information on the USS Vermont - She was primarily used as a receiving ship a stationary vessel used to house new naval recruits and personnel awaiting assignment. But couldn't find any record of it being in San Francisco at the time. She spent most of her service in the New York Navy Yard.

Ships. In the 4 to 8 PM Events of the Day log entry, it says that 22 men were sent to San Francisco for transportation to US Receiving Ship Vermont. It does not say if they are being transfered to that ship or even specifically where the ship is.

10:00 AM: Moored. Moored USS Lackawanna along side Navy Yard dock.
Ships: Ships. At 10:00 AM sent a working party to the 'Jamestown' to assist in unmooring her. I believe they did this to make room so they could moor USS Lackawanna alongside the Navy Yard dock. The USS Jamestown was inactive at the time and decommissioned in 1871. It was recommissioned and transitioned into a training vessel in 1876.

10:45 AM: Sent to Navigation Store House, the Astronomical instruments used in observation of the transit of Venus. There were expeditons to the southern hemisphere in the Pacific for the 1874 transit of Venus, but there are no records of the USS Lackawanna being involved. It could be that they were just transporting the equipment used.
